parentQuestion: Vital centers for the control of heart rate, respiration, and blood pressure are...

scaffolds.q: Your heart rate and breathing are controlled by your brain even when you're unconscious.
scaffolds.explain: The brainstem contains automatic control centers that regulate heart rate, breathing, and blood pressure 24/7, regardless of consciousness level. You can be completely unconscious, and your brainstem is still running the show. this is where the magic happens.

scaffolds.q: Which brainstem region is closest to and continuous with the spinal cord?
scaffolds.explain: The medulla oblongata is the lowest part of the brainstem, directly continuous with the spinal cord at the foramen magnum — the opening at the base of your skull. Let me show you why this location is so critical.
